request.question: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?

title: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes
Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)
Jay Weinberg
Ed Manion
Frank Stefanko
The Fever (Bruce Springsteen song)
Hesitation Wounds
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09
Hesitation Marks
Hesitation Wounds (album)
sentences: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes are a Jersey Shore musical group led by Southside Johnny.
They have been recording albums since 1976 and are closely associated with Bruce Springsteen & the E Street Band.
They have recorded and/or performed several Springsteen songs, including "The Fever" and "Fade Away".
Springsteen has also performed with the band on numerous occasions and in 1991 guested on their "Better Days" album.

During the band's formative years Miami Steve Van Zandt acted as the band's co-leader, guitarist, songwriter, arranger and producer while other E Streeters including Clarence Clemons, Max Weinberg, Garry Tallent, Ernest Carter, Patti Scialfa and Soozie Tyrell have all performed, toured and/or recorded with the Jukes.
The band's horn section – the Miami Horns – has also toured and recorded with Springsteen.
More than one hundred musicians can claim to have been members of the Asbury Jukes, including Jon Bon Jovi who toured with the band as a special guest during 1990.
Bon Jovi has also cited the band as an influence and Jukes' Bobby Bandiera and Jeff Kazee have also toured with Bon Jovi.

Other notable band members include Mark Pender and Richie "La Bamba" Rosenberg who have played regularly with the Max Weinberg 7 on both "Late Night with Conan O'Brien" and "The Tonight Show with Conan O'Brien".

"Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)" is a 1973 song by Bruce Springsteen, from his "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle" album, and is especially famed as a concert number for Springsteen and The E Street Band.
The song, which clocks in at just over seven minutes, is a story of forbidden love between the singer and the titular Rosalita, whose parents disapprove of his life in a rock and roll band.
It is included on the compilation albums "The Essential Bruce Springsteen" and "Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band Greatest Hits".
Jay Weinberg (born September 8, 1990), is an American musician and drummer.
He is the son of American drummer Max Weinberg.
He has played with the American punk band the Reveling and toured in 2009 as a drummer with Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band, substituting for his father.
During 2010, he was briefly the drummer for Madball.
During 2011 and 2012, Weinberg played with Against Me!
In 2014, Weinberg became the drummer for the American heavy metal music band Slipknot.

Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.
Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.
He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.
As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.
Frank Stefanko is a fine art photographer with connections to New Jersey performers Patti Smith and Bruce Springsteen.
Stefanko's early photographs, taken in the 1960s through the 1980s, reveal the emerging careers of the two young artists.
Frank retains an on-going working relationship with both Springsteen and Smith.
Stefanko will be releasing a book in November of 2017, entitled "Bruce Springsteen: Further Up the Road" that chronicles the 40-year working relationship between himself and Bruce Springsteen.

"The Fever" is an early Bruce Springsteen song, recorded and released on an extremely rare 7" demo to promote the band, coupled with 'Rendezvous', a live track recorded in New York, although the venue and date remain a mystery - Rendezvous opens with Bruce calling out "New York!
Go ahead, Max!"
The single was released under The Jersey Devil and was pressed on Bruce Records, which was Springsteen's own independent label for his band.
The Fever itself was performed by Springsteen in concert beginning in March 1973.

It was recorded in 914 Sound Studios in May 1973, during sessions for Springsteen's second album "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle", but was only used as a demo by manager Mike Appel's Laurel Canyon publishing arm and renamed "Fever For the Girl".
In early 1974 Appel sent it to several progressive rock radio stations, where it became a "turntable hit" in the ramp-up of Springsteen expectations prior to "Born to Run".

The original Springsteen recording finally achieved an official release surfacing in 1999 on his "18 Tracks" outtakes collection- the live b-side has never been officially released since in any other format, although a different live version is on 18 Tracks and Tracks (Bruce Springsteen album) and a studio cut of Rendezvous was released on 'The Promise' CD in 2010.
Hesitation Wounds is an American hardcore punk supergroup formed in 2012.

The band features vocalist Jeremy Bolm of Touché Amoré; guitarist Neeraj Kane of The Hope Conspiracy and formerly of The Suicide File and Holy Fever; bassist Stephen LaCour of True Cross and formerly of Trap Them; and Jay Weinberg, the son of Max Weinberg (E Street Band, The Max Weinberg 7), who previously performed with Madball and Against Me!
, and eventually joined Slipknot.
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09 is a live album by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, released on July 13, 2017.
It is the fourteenth such release by the Bruce Springsteen Archives.

The performance was Springsteen's final show at the soon to be demolished Wachovia Spectrum in Philadelphia, PA and featured a full album performance of "Born in the U.S.A.", the show opener, "The Price You Pay", which was performed for the first time since 1981 and guest appearances by former band member Vini Lopez and even Springsteen's mother Adele.
Hesitation Marks is the eighth studio album by American industrial rock band Nine Inch Nails, released on August 30, 2013 by Columbia Records.
It is the band's first release through Columbia, and their first album released on a major record label since "Year Zero" (2007).
The album's title is derived from the term "hesitation wounds", which is used to describe marks that are produced by testing a bladed weapon before attempting suicide or self-harming.
Hesitation Wounds is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Sleep, a member of the Pacific Northwest hip hop collective Oldominion.
It was released on Strange Famous Records on June 29, 2009.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question` and `passages`, produce the single field `summary`.

Goal: compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a short intermediate state that makes it easy for the next-hop agent to (a) see what facts are present per candidate/entity, (b) see what is ambiguous or missing, and (c) know 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. This module does not produce the final answer unless the provided passages already unambiguously contain every fact required to resolve the question's relation for every argument.

Procedure:
1) Decide whether the question's relation is semantically complete given the supplied passages. Treat the hop as INCOMPLETE if any of the following hold: a) the relation requires combining or comparing facts across pages (e.g., comparing birth dates, publication/founding years, ordinal ranks, confirming a named director among candidates) and the passages do not supply each required fact unambiguously for all relevant arguments; b) any relation argument is ambiguous or has multiple candidate pages or mentions in the passages; c) an expected candidate page or explicit fact for an argument is missing (e.g., only one side of a comparison is present). Do not trigger INCOMPLETE solely from surface tokens (years, digits) — base the decision on whether the relation's arguments are unambiguously resolved.

2) If the hop is COMPLETE (all required facts to resolve the relation for every argument are present unambiguously in the passages): produce a concise derived result in `summary`. This may be a short phrase or sentence that states the necessary fact(s) needed by the pipeline. Keep it minimal and grounded in the passages; you may include a 1–2 token citation (passage title) if helpful.

3) If the hop is INCOMPLETE or AMBIGUOUS: do NOT assert a final answer or perform the final comparison/selection. Instead emit a short structured report in `summary` containing these labeled elements (prefer bullets or a compact paragraph with explicit labels). Keep all text neutral and non-assertive.

  - evidence: For each named candidate/argument mentioned in the question or passages, list the candidate label and the exact passage title(s) that mention it plus a very short quoted snippet or explicit fact token found there (1–12 words). If a candidate is not mentioned, say so briefly.

  - ambiguities_or_missing: Explicitly name any ambiguities (e.g., multiple candidates for the same name, multiple pages that could be the intended entity) and any missing expected pages or facts.

  - missing_facts_or_bridge: State exactly which fact(s) or disambiguation is needed to complete the relation (e.g., "birth date for Person A", "publication year of Work B", "director credit for Film C"). Be as specific and minimal as possible.

  - retrieval_direction: Provide 1–4 prioritized next-hop targets or concise queries to resolve the missing facts/ambiguities. Prefer explicit page titles when available (exact if known), otherwise provide short search-style queries (few words) or a clear description of the document to retrieve. Order targets by priority.

Formatting and style constraints (apply to both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE cases):
- The output must be a single `summary` field only. Do not emit other fields.
- In INCOMPLETE reports, use neutral, non-assertive phrasing. Do not produce final comparisons, numeric answers, or definitive selections (e.g., avoid "X is the oldest" or "Answer: 3").
- Keep the report compact and actionable (short labeled bullets or one concise paragraph). Avoid long prose.
- It is allowed and encouraged to quote short factual snippets from passages and cite passage titles to show grounding.
- Do not defer or mark INCOMPLETE when the passages already unambiguously provide all facts for the relation and its arguments.

Produce the `summary` content that follows these rules.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the single field `query`.

Goal
- Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-intent query suitable for a second-hop search that will recover the missing bridging evidence needed to answer the question. Do not produce the final answer in `query`.

Semantic decision rule (when to emit a retrieval-intent query)
- If summary_1 does NOT assert a confirmed linking entity or verified bridging fact needed to answer the question, you MUST emit exactly one compact retrieval-intent query. "Does not assert a confirmed bridge" includes short or candidate-only summaries (e.g., a single named entity, a quoted candidate title, or numeric anchors that are not verified) and any case where the visible anchors do not unambiguously identify the needed bridge.
- If summary_1 already names a confirmed bridge (a clearly identified, verified linking entity or unambiguous metadata that directly connects the question's pieces), you may produce a compact query to fetch supporting evidence, but do NOT produce an answer in the `query` field.
- If summary_1 explicitly states that the bridge cannot be recovered automatically (extreme ambiguity or user-local context requiring clarification), do NOT force a misleading retrieval query. In that case, set `query` to the single short token BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE (all caps, no punctuation) so downstream steps can prompt the user for clarification. Do not invent facts.

Query construction rules
- Output exactly one compact line of text as `query`. Keep it short and retrieval-focused (few keywords, no full sentences). Use visible anchors from `question` and `summary_1` and minimal retrieval keywords that express the missing relation (e.g., anchor1 anchor2 which artist recorded both; AlbumTitle album artist genre; 1991–92 NHL season regular season).
- Do not include the final answer, assertions, or user-facing instructions in the `query` field. Avoid polite phrasing, questions to the user, or explanatory text. Prefer noun phrases and relation keywords.
- If your natural impulse would be to write a declarative answer or a clarification sentence in `query`, instead convert that output into a compact retrieval-intent query that targets the missing bridge.
- Preserve semantic fidelity: base the query only on visible anchors and relations in `question` and `summary_1`. Do not invent hidden identifiers or assume unseen facts.

Format contract
- Return exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short string. If unrecoverable as described above, set `query` to BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Examples of disallowed outputs for `query`
- A declarative answer (e.g., "The artist was X.")
- A user-facing clarification sentence (e.g., "Do you mean X or Y?") unless summary_1 explicitly requires clarification — in that case use BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Behavioral constraints
- Do not use surface heuristics (token count, punctuation, or quotation marks) as the trigger; apply the semantic rule above.
- Emit only one compact retrieval string when the bridge is missing; do not emit multiple queries or long explanations.

Inputs available: `question`, `summary_1`. Output required: single field `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`. The `summary` must be a concise, post-retrieval integration of the evidence that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Follow these rules when composing `summary`:

- Purpose: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` into a grounded state that retains or resolves the earlier bridge only when the second-hop passages supply relation- or attribute-facts that are necessary to answer or disambiguate the `question` (for example: partnership/membership links, joint-award partners indicated by 'with', or requested biographical qualifiers such as nationality, birthdate, current team/status).

- When to append supporting sentence(s): Only append short passage-grounded supporting sentence(s) when the question's semantic intent requires relation or attribute facts to answer or disambiguate. In those cases, produce a compact integrated summary that (a) preserves the concise primary answer or canonical label/token chosen from `context`, and (b) appends one short supporting sentence per relevant entity (or one joint sentence for explicit partnerships) that is directly supported by the supplied `passages` and identity-aligned to the entity referenced in `context`.

- When not to append: Do not append supporting sentences and instead return only the canonical label/token when any of the following apply: the question expects a normalized identifier or single token (examples: binary/multiple-choice label selection between named options, identification-only questions, or explicit token-extraction such as 'middle name'); or the `context` already contains a full, passage-consistent, multi-clause integrated answer. These are semantic exceptions — do not append merely because an answer in `context` is short.

- Identity alignment and verification: Before appending, confirm that the fact in `passages` refers to the same entity referenced in `context` (match names, common aliases, roles, dates, or other corroborating attributes). If identity is ambiguous or unsupported, refrain from appending and preserve uncertainty instead.

- Style and content constraints: Keep output concise. If appending, limit `summary` to 1–3 sentences total (the primary concise answer plus at most one short supporting sentence per relevant entity or one joint supporting sentence). Avoid inventing facts, avoid long verbatim quotations from `passages`, and avoid name-collision errors. Do not change which label or name is selected by the existing answer-selection logic.

- If there is insufficient information in `context` and `passages` to resolve the needed relation/attribute, summarize the uncertainty succinctly rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Produce only the `summary` field as the integrated output string.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use only summary_1 and summary_2 as evidence. Treat summary_2 as the later, integrated evidence and prioritize it when reconciling information; use summary_1 only as the earlier, pre-second-hop context. Do not assume the final state is unresolved solely because summary_1 contains bridging or retrieval-oriented language.

Produce exactly one field, answer, whose value is the minimal final answer string that is fully supported by the visible summaries. The answer field must contain only that minimal plain-text string and nothing else (no explanation, no surrounding quotes, no JSON wrappers, no debugging or transport artifacts).

Decide whether the question's intent is binary (a truth-value / yes-no question) or non-binary (asking for a name, title, date, place, numeric count, selection, or other factual value). Make this decision semantically (based on the question's meaning), not by surface heuristics like token counts or punctuation.

- If the question's intent is binary AND both summary_1 and summary_2 unambiguously agree on the same truth-value, emit exactly one lowercase token: either yes or no (and nothing else) as the answer value.

- For all other question intents (non-binary), emit the minimal factual string supported by the summaries exactly as needed: preserve multi-token values, necessary punctuation, full date formats, and place formats (for example, full names, "City, State", or "Month Day, Year"), and do not truncate these to a single token.

If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not jointly support an unambiguous minimal answer, do not fabricate or over-assert; follow the system's established fallback behavior for ambiguous or unsupported questions.

Always forbid emitting serialized queries, JSON/dict-like structures, internal-debug wrappers, or other transport artifacts as the final answer. The answer must be plain text only.

Make all judgments about minimality and agreement based on the semantic content of summary_1 and summary_2, with priority to summary_2 when they differ.

Produce the answer value exactly as specified above in the answer field.

Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

Stefanko's early photographs, taken in the 1960s through the 1980s, reveal the emerging careers of the two young artists.
Frank retains an on-going working relationship with both Springsteen and Smith.

, and eventually joined Slipknot.

response.hop1_query: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?
hop1_docs: Working on a Dream Tour | The Working on a Dream Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, which began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009.  It followed the late January 2009 release of the album "Working on a Dream".  This was the first full E Street Band tour without founding member Danny Federici, who died during the previous tour in 2008, and the final tour for founding member Clarence Clemons, who died in 2011.

Ed Manion | Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.  Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.  He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.  As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.

Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour | The Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour was a concert tour featuring Bruce Springsteen and a new backing band, that took place from mid-1992 to mid-1993.  It followed the simultaneous release of his albums "Human Touch" and "Lucky Town" earlier in 1992.  It was his first of four non-E Street Band tours.  Later, Springsteen had more non-E Street Band tours, the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour, the Seeger Sessions Tour, and the Devils & Dust Tour.  The tour was not as commercially or critically successful as past tours, due to poor reception of Human Touch and Lucky Town as well as changes from previous tours.  According to Springsteen biographer Dave Marsh, die-hard fans have informally referred to the backing band as "the Other Band" (and the tour as "The Other Band Tour").
Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour | Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour | Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour

Wrecking Ball World Tour | The Wrecking Ball World Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band to promote Springsteen's seventeenth studio album, "Wrecking Ball", which was released on March 5, 2012.  It was the first tour for the E Street Band without founding member Clarence Clemons, who died on June 18, 2011.  The world-wide tour in support of the album, which ended in September 2013, reached 26 countries, the most ever for one of Springsteen's tours.  The tour was resumed in January 2014 however this time it was to promote his new album, "High Hopes" and went under that album's name.
Magic Tour (Bruce Springsteen) | The Magic Tour was Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band's 2007–2008 concert tour of North America and Western Europe.
hop1_titles: Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour
response.summary_1:
- evidence:
  - Hesitation Wounds: NOT MENTIONED in provided passages (no band page or member list present).
  - Bruce Springsteen 2009 tour: "Working on a Dream Tour ... began in April 2009" (Passage 1).
  - Ed Manion: "touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band" (Passage 2) — no connection to Hesitation Wounds shown.

- ambiguities_or_missing:
  - Ambiguous which Hesitation Wounds member is meant (no members listed in passages).
  - No passage states any Hesitation Wounds member toured with Springsteen in 2009 (missing explicit tie and year confirmation).

- missing_facts_or_bridge:
  - Full list of Hesitation Wounds band members (names).
  - For each member, any touring credits linking them to Bruce Springsteen or the E Street Band, and confirmation that the touring occurred in 2009 (or specifically on the Working on a Dream Tour).

- retrieval_direction (priority order):
  1. "Hesitation Wounds — Wikipedia" (or official band page) to get member names.
  2. Individual pages or bios for each Hesitation Wounds member (search: "[Member Name] Wikipedia" or "[Member Name] biography") to check touring credits.
  3. "Working on a Dream Tour personnel" or "Bruce Springsteen 2009 touring musicians / horn section 2009" to verify which musicians toured in 2009.
  4. (If needed) search query: "Hesitation Wounds [member name] Bruce Springsteen 2009" or "[member name] toured with Bruce Springsteen" for news/press mentions.
response.hop2_query: Hesitation Wounds band members Bruce Springsteen 2009 tour

As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.

Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour | The Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour was a concert tour featuring Bruce Springsteen and a new backing band, that took place from mid-1992 to mid-1993.  It followed the simultaneous release of his albums "Human Touch" and "Lucky Town" earlier in 1992.  It was his first of four non-E Street Band tours.  Later, Springsteen had more non-E Street Band tours, the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour, the Seeger Sessions Tour, and the Devils & Dust Tour.  The tour was not as commercially or critically successful as past tours, due to poor reception of Human Touch and Lucky Town as well as changes from previous tours.  According to Springsteen biographer Dave Marsh, die-hard fans have informally referred to the backing band as "the Other Band" (and the tour as "The Other Band Tour").
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour | Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour
Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour | Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Magic Tour (Bruce Springsteen) | The Magic Tour was Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band's 2007–2008 concert tour of North America and Western Europe.

Wrecking Ball World Tour | The Wrecking Ball World Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band to promote Springsteen's seventeenth studio album, "Wrecking Ball", which was released on March 5, 2012.  It was the first tour for the E Street Band without founding member Clarence Clemons, who died on June 18, 2011.  The world-wide tour in support of the album, which ended in September 2013, reached 26 countries, the most ever for one of Springsteen's tours.  The tour was resumed in January 2014 however this time it was to promote his new album, "High Hopes" and went under that album's name.

response.summary_2: Insufficient information to answer: the provided passages confirm Bruce Springsteen's Working on a Dream Tour took place in 2009 and that Ed Manion has toured with Springsteen, but none of the passages list Hesitation Wounds members or link any Hesitation Wounds member to touring with Springsteen in 2009.
